zh.lifeSummary: 當John Tucker於1545年出生在英國倫敦時，他的父親John William Tucker25歲，母親Jane Haimor25歲。他於1580年5月8日與Alice Pelham在英國米德萊可倫敦 聖 克萊門特 易亞斯亞普 煙雨 聖 馬丁 奧爾加結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1602年享年57歲，埋葬在英國倫敦。

nameMeanings.name: Tucker
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southwestern): occupational name from Middle English tuker(e), toker(e) ‘tucker, fuller’, a derivative of tuken ‘to torment, beat’ (Old English tūcian), for someone who fulled and finished cloth. This name for the occupation was characteristic of the West Country. Compare Fuller and Walker and see also Tuckerman .
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tuachair ‘descendant of Tuachar’, a personal name composed of the elements tuath ‘people’ + car ‘dear, beloved’.
Americanized form of Jewish Tocker or Toker (see Tokarz ).

variants.name: Tooker
meanings: English (Devon) and Irish: variant of   Tucker  .

variants.name: Tuckerman
meanings: English: from Middle English tuker(e), toker(e) ‘tucker, fuller’ (see Tucker 1) + -man, either for a tucker, or for his servant or assistant.

variants.name: Fuller
meanings: English: occupational name for a dresser of cloth, from Middle English fuller ‘fuller of cloth’ (partly from Old English fullere, partly from Old French fouleor, foleur, Latin fullo). Raw cloth had to be fulled, i.e. scoured, cleansed, and thickened by beating or trampling it in water, a process also known as walking or tucking, hence the surnames Walker and Tucker alongside Fuller. These three terms and surnames are characteristic of different parts of England. In general, in Middle English, Fuller is southern and eastern, while Walker belongs to the west and north and Tucker is southwestern. Compare Fullen .
English: variant of Fullard with loss of -d.
German (Füller): occupational name for a person whose work involved filling, such as a dauber, or a nickname for a gourmand or glutton. Compare Filler .

variants.name: Swinger
meanings: English: occupational name, perhaps for a fuller, who finished cloth by beating it and stretching it on tenters. Middle English swyngere is not independently recorded but it appears to be an agent derivative of Middle English swingen ‘to beat, strike, punish, afflict (something)’. Compare the synonymous Tucker , an agent derivative of Middle English tuken ‘to torment, beat’. The earliest bearers of the name Swinger were wealthy townsmen.
In some instances probably an Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) Schwinger , or German Zwinger, a nickname from Middle High German zwinger ‘oppressor’.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When John Tucker was born in 1545, in London, England, his father, John William Tucker, was 25 and his mother, Jane Haimor, was 25. He married Alice Pelham on 8 May 1580, in London St Clement Eastcheap with St Martin Orgar, Middlesex, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1602, at the age of 57, and was buried in London, England.

Il a épousé Alice Pelham le 8 mai 1580, à London St Clement Eastcheap with St Martin Orgar, Middlesex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Lui sposato Alice Pelham il 8 maggio 1580, nel London St Clement Eastcheap with St Martin Orgar, Middlesex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. ko.lifeSummary: John Tucker(이)가 1545년 영국 런던에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John William Tucker(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Jane Haimor(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1580년 5월 8일 영국 미들레세스 런던 스트 클레멘트 이스트체아프 위스 스트 마틴 르가에서 Alice Pelham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1602년 57세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 런던에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Tucker nasceu em 1545, em London, Inglaterra, seu pai, John William Tucker, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Jane Haimor, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Alice Pelham em 8 de maio de 1580, em London St Clement Eastcheap with St Martin Orgar, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1602, com 57 anos, e foi sepultado em London, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Tucker родился в 1545 г. в местности Лондон, Англия, его отцу по имени John William Tucker было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Jane Haimor, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Pelham 8 мая 1580 г. в местности лондон ст клемент эасчип вит ст мартинь оргар, миддлесекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1602 г. в возрасте 57 лет и был похоронен в местности Лондон, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Tucker nació en 1545, en London, Inglaterra, su papá, John William Tucker, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Jane Haimor, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Alice Pelham el 8 de mayo de 1580, en London St Clement Eastcheap with St Martin Orgar, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1602, a la edad de 57 años, y fue sepultado en London, Inglaterra.
